own_review_points: getXMLExtensionJars resolves jar path relative to the CONTRIBUTING bundle (getContributor().getName()) which is correct, but javadoc claims paths are relative to plugin root - needs clarification; human reviewer suspects most resolutions will be erroneous
new XMLExtensionRegistry() on every getExtensionJarPaths() call registers a registry change listener that is never removed -> listener leak
extensions HashMap/HashSet and outOfSync boolean are accessed by the registry-change listener thread and the LS thread without synchronization -> data race
.endsWith(.jar) filter on getValue() (the path) could be simplified
FileLocator.find failure path logs but drops the offending plugin id / path
schema .exsd example section and PR description use the wrong fully-qualified id XMLExtensions instead of xmllsExtension
notes: v8 leads with a FABRICATED critical: it claims extension.getAttribute(path) reads the <extension> parent and returns null so no jars load. In Eclipse, getConfigurationElementsFor(pointId) returns the <jar> CHILD elements, which DO carry the path attribute, so the code works - wrong (-1, fab). Point #4 says RELEASE_NOTES.md shows the wrong id XMLExtensions, but the added RELEASE_NOTES content actually uses the correct xmllsExtension; the wrong id lives in the PR description and the .exsd example - wrong (-1, fab). Good points: listener leak (+1), thread-safety (+1), .exsd example wrong-id (+1), empty doc blocks (+1), lost log context (+1). Missed the human reviewer's CENTRAL point - jar path resolution being relative to the contributing plugin/erroneous - and the .jar-filter simplification. Net mildly positive but undermined by a flashy false critical.
